hypothesis: Incorporating a Force-Delta consistency penalty λ||Fnc + ∇E||^2 into fine-tuning of pre-trained direct-force NNIPs on a small (≤1% of original) task-specific DFT set will produce a near-linear calibration between UΔ and εdirect (R^2 ≥ 0.8) on adversarial OOD structures and reduce MD instability events by at least 50% relative to fine-tuning without the penalty at matched data and compute budgets.
